# Strata

> A themeable, accessible design system built layer by layer — a two-tier W3C design-token architecture and 21 Lit 3 web components with typed React 19 and Vue 3 adapters, documented in this Storybook. Built by Hiten Patel (https://hiten.dev).

## Architecture

- Tokens: single W3C Design Tokens source of truth (`tokens/tokens.json`), two tiers — primitives (colour ramps, spacing, radii, shadows, motion) and semantic role aliases (`surface`, `text-muted`, `accent`, `danger-subtle`) defined per theme.
- Themes: light (default) and dark via `data-theme`; every semantic token re-mapped, components never reference primitives directly.
- Variants: alternate accent layered on either theme (`data-variant="showcase"`) — brand theming without touching component code.
- Components: Lit 3 web components consuming only `--strata-*` semantic custom properties, so they respond to theme/variant switches for free.
- Adapters: typed React wrappers via @lit/react and native Vue 3 support.

## Accessibility

- WCAG 2.2 AA contrast in both themes; forced-colors mode mapped to system colours
- Designed `:focus-visible` states — two-layer ring (2px surface, 4px focus colour), never removed
- 40px minimum interactive targets; `prefers-reduced-motion` honoured
- Roving tabindex + arrow-key patterns (Tabs, Menu, RadioGroup); Escape dismisses overlays
- Every component ships axe-checked tests (@open-wc/testing); 170+ tests run in CI

## Use it

- npm: `npm install @hitenpatel/strata`
- Live Storybook: https://ui.hiten.dev
- Author: Hiten Patel — senior frontend engineer, https://hiten.dev
- Full CV: https://hiten.dev/llms.txt and https://ask.hiten.dev
